mnemonics.title: ABAC Components
mnemonics.content: PEP intercepts, PDP decides, PAP manages policies, PIP provides attributes - 'Please Enforce Decisions Promptly, Administer Policies Properly' or 'Every Dog Plays And Prances' (PEP, PDP, PAP, PIP)

mnemonics.title: Four Attribute Categories
mnemonics.content: SREA - Subject, Resource, Environment, Action - 'Subjects Requesting Extra Access'

mnemonics.title: ABAC vs RBAC
mnemonics.content: RBAC is 'Roles Are Static' (rigid), ABAC is 'Attributes Bring Adaptive Control' (flexible)

acronyms.usage: Authorization model using attributes of subject, resource, action, and environment to make access decisions
XML-based standard language for expressing ABAC policies
Component that intercepts access requests and enforces access decisions
Component that evaluates policies and makes access decisions
Component for creating and managing access control policies
Component that retrieves and provides attribute values for policy evaluation
Traditional access control model using static role assignments

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: User → PEP (request) → PIP (get attributes) → PDP (evaluate) → PEP (enforce decision) → User (result). Place PAP and PIP as supporting boxes on the sides.
Create a 2x2 grid: Top-left=Subject (who), Top-right=Resource (what), Bottom-left=Action (how), Bottom-right=Environment (when/where). Fill each with examples.
Think of a security checkpoint: PEP is the guard who checks your request, PDP is the control room that says yes/no, PIP is the database that verifies your ID details.

analogies.analogy: If RBAC is like giving fixed hotel key cards (same access for all in a role), ABAC is like a smart room system that checks your role, time of day, room type, and loyalty status to decide what you can access.
Think of a restaurant: PEP is the waiter taking your order, PDP is the chef deciding if you can have that dish, PAP is the menu creator (management), PIP is the pantry providing ingredient details.

Like a movie ticket system: Subject (who you are - student/senior), Resource (which screen/seat), Action (watch in 3D/IMAX), Environment (matinee/evening show, weekday/weekend) - all determine your ticket price and access.
